insight: For Senegal, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, the team has experienced a slight decline in momentum after breaks, with a mean change of -0.050 per minute, indicating that they tend to lose a bit of steam after stopping play. However, the numbers also suggest that when Senegal is not pressing before a break, they are able to maintain their momentum, with no change in average momentum after the break, whereas when they are pressing, they tend to lose more momentum, with an average decline of 0.100 per minute. This suggests that Senegal may need to adjust their strategy when pressing before a hydration break to avoid losing their edge during the stoppage.
